item.title: UCB signs agreement to strengthen carbon accounting
item.description: United Commercial Bank PLC (UCB) has signed an agreement with Ernst & Young Advisory Services Bangladesh Limited (EY ASBL) to further enhance its carbon accounting and carbon emission measurement practices. The agreement was formally signed recently at UCB’s Corporate Office. It is being con

item.title: Prime Bank & Mastercard launch next-gen NFC sticker card ‘TapIn’ to redefine payment experience
item.description: Prime Bank PLC. in partnership with Mastercard, has launched `TapIn', an innovative next generation NFC-enabled debit sticker card developed, powered by IDEMIA, with local partner ADN Technologies Ltd. designed to deliver fast, secure and seamless payment experience for customers with dynamic urban

item.title: Melita Mehjabeen elected chairman of National Bank
item.description: Prof Melita Mehjabeen has been elected chairman of private lender National Bank, replacing Environment, Forests and Climate Change Minister Abdul Awal Mintoo. The decision was made at the bank’s 539th board meeting held on Saturday, according to information published on the bank’s webs

item.title: Heads of Sonali Bank, IDRA, and SBC resign citing personal reasons
item.description: In a significant shake-up of the financial sector, the heads of three major state-linked financial institutions—Sonali Bank, Insurance Development and Regulatory Authority (IDRA), and Shadharan Bima Corporation (SBC)—resigned from their respective posts on Monday. The outgoing official

item.title: Nurul Islam Moni made chief whip
item.description: BNP lawmaker Nurul Islam Moni MP of Bagura-2 constituency has been appointed as chief whip of the 13th national parliament. A gazette notification was issued by the National Parliament Secretariat as per the order of the President on Monday. Besides, six more MPs were appointed as whips. Separate

item.title: 8 injured in Mohammadpur gas cylinder explosion
item.description: A woman among eight people has been injured in an explosion linked to a gas cylinder leak in Dhaka’s Mohammadpur. The incident occurred around 2pm on Humayun Road on Monday. Mohammadpur Police chief Mezbah Uddin said the explosion was believed to have been caused by a gas leak from a cylind

item.title: Eid bus, train ticket sales begin Tuesday
item.description: Bus and train operators have announced that the sale of tickets for the Eid-ul-Fitr holiday rush begins on Tuesday. While bus tickets will be available both at counters and online, Bangladesh Railway has maintained its policy of selling 100 percent of its advance tickets online. The railway has d

item.title: Israel’s Ben Gurion Airport set to reopen airspace in very limited format on Monday
item.description: Israel’s Ben Gurion Airport, the country’s main international gateway near Tel Aviv, said Israeli airspace was set to reopen as early as Monday evening for civilian flights in an “extremely limited format”. Starting on Tuesday, “flight operations will gradually expand

item.title: Nahid questions interim advisor’s foreign ministry role
item.description: National Citizen Party (NCP) Convenor Nahid Islam has once again raised questions over the appointment of an interim government advisor to lead the foreign ministry. He addressed the issue on Monday during an Iftar event hosted by the NCP in Chattogram. Nahid, a member of parliament, said: "Our s

item.title: Tarique cancels Bogura visit due to by-election
item.description: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman has cancelled his scheduled visit to Bogura on Mar 10, where he was set to start the distribution of Family Card. Bogura Deputy Commissioner Toufikur Rahman confirmed the visit’s cancellation on Monday night. "The prime minister has opted not to conduct the pro

item.title: Qatar LNG, Saudi refinery, Israeli oil, gas fields down due to Mideast strikes
item.description: Qatar halted its production of liquefied natural gas on Monday, as Iran continued to strike Gulf countries in retaliation for Israeli and US strikes against it, prompting precautionary shutdowns of oil and gas facilities across the Middle East. Qatari LNG production is equivalent to about 20 perce

item.title: UK PM says Iran stepping up 'reckless' strikes after Khamenei’s killing
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Monday that the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei has led Iran to strike military and economic targets in a more "reckless" manner in the region. The death of Khamenei "will not stop Iran from launching these strikes," Starmer told parliament
